title: Spoiled Sports The Dangers of Steroids
titleZh: 被毁的运动：类固醇的危害

q.en: In 2013, what did cyclist Lance Armstrong finally admit?
q.zh: 2013年，自行车运动员兰斯·阿姆斯特朗最终承认了什么？

options.en: He never won the Tour de France
options.zh: 他从未赢得过环法自行车赛

options.en: His 'perfect story' was 'one big lie' because he used steroids
options.zh: 他的“完美故事”是“一个大谎言”，因为他使用了类固醇

options.en: He was never diagnosed with cancer
He had always followed the rules

explain.en: Armstrong admitted in 2013 that his 'mythic, perfect story' was 'one big lie' after years of denial that he took steroids.
explain.zh: 阿姆斯特朗在否认多年后，于2013年承认他“神话般的完美故事”是“一个大谎言”，因为他使用了类固醇。

q.en: Anabolic-androgenic steroids (AAS) are similar to which natural substance?
q.zh: 合成代谢雄激素类固醇（AAS）与哪种天然物质相似？

explain.en: The book states anabolic steroids are similar to the hormone testosterone, which the body produces naturally in small amounts.
explain.zh: 书中说明合成代谢类固醇与人体自然少量分泌的睾酮激素相似。

q.en: In what year did the International Olympic Committee ban the use of steroids?
q.zh: 国际奥委会在哪一年禁止使用类固醇？

explain.en: So many athletes were using steroids that the IOC, which organizes the Olympics, banned their use in 1975.
explain.zh: 由于太多运动员使用类固醇，主办奥运会的国际奥委会于1975年禁止使用。

q.en: Which baseball stars were refused entry into the Baseball Hall of Fame because of steroid use?
q.zh: 哪些棒球明星因使用类固醇而被拒绝进入棒球名人堂？

options.zh: 马克·麦圭尔和巴里·邦兹
罗杰·马里斯和贝比·鲁斯
萨米·索萨和德里克·杰特
杰基·罗宾逊和汉克·阿伦

explain.en: The book says McGwire, Bonds, and other top stars have been refused induction into the Hall of Fame and branded cheaters.
explain.zh: 书中说麦圭尔、邦兹等顶级球星被拒绝进入名人堂，并被贴上作弊者的标签。

q.en: What does the term 'roid rage' refer to?
q.zh: 术语“roid rage”（类固醇暴怒）指的是什么？

options.en: Overly aggressive behavior caused by steroids
options.zh: 由类固醇引起的过度攻击性行为

explain.en: Steroids can cause panic attacks, depression, and an overly aggressive behavior informally called 'roid rage.'
explain.zh: 类固醇会导致恐慌发作、抑郁以及一种被通俗称为“roid rage”的过度攻击性行为。

q.en: For adolescents, what serious effect can steroid use cause?
q.zh: 对青少年来说，使用类固醇可能导致什么严重后果？

explain.en: The glossary defines 'stunted' as limited in growth; adolescents who use steroids can have severe side effects such as stunted growth.
explain.zh: 词汇表将“stunted”定义为生长受限；使用类固醇的青少年可能出现生长迟缓等严重副作用。

q.en: What does blood doping do to the body?
q.zh: 血液兴奋剂（blood doping）对身体有什么作用？

options.en: Increases the oxygen carried by red blood cells
options.zh: 增加红细胞携带的氧气量

explain.en: Blood doping increases the amount of oxygen carried by the body's red blood cells, making the body run more efficiently.
explain.zh: 血液兴奋剂增加红细胞携带的氧气量，使身体运转更高效。

q.en: What happened to sprinter Ben Johnson after the 1988 Olympics?
q.zh: 短跑运动员本·约翰逊在1988年奥运会后发生了什么？

options.en: He had to return his gold medal after testing positive
options.zh: 药检呈阳性后被迫交回金牌

options.en: He set a new world record
options.zh: 他创造了新的世界纪录

explain.en: Ben Johnson won the 100-meter sprint at the 1988 Olympics but later tested positive for steroids and had to return his gold medal.
explain.zh: 本·约翰逊赢得1988年奥运会100米短跑金牌，但后来类固醇药检呈阳性，被迫交回金牌。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in the Conclusion section?
q.zh: 作者在“结论”部分的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To list steroid policies by sport
options.zh: 按运动项目列出类固醇政策

options.en: To warn that steroids change who you are and leave lasting harm
options.zh: 警示类固醇会改变人的本质并留下持久的伤害

options.en: To teach how to build muscle safely

explain.en: The Conclusion says steroids change who you are and leave terrible side effects that may never subside, warning of the personal cost of cheating.
explain.zh: 结论说类固醇会改变人的本质，留下可能永不消退的副作用，警示作弊的个人代价。

q.en: Why does the author say teens take steroids, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，作者认为青少年为何使用类固醇？

options.en: They are required by their schools
They are influenced by media ideals of body image and competitive school sports
options.zh: 受媒体对身体形象的审美观和高竞争校运影响

options.en: Doctors prescribe them to all teens
options.zh: 医生给所有青少年开这种药

explain.en: Researchers say teens take steroids because of the media's perception of a beautiful body and the highly competitive nature of school sports.
explain.zh: 研究者认为青少年使用类固醇是受媒体对身体美的观念以及校运高度竞争的影响。

prompt.en: What would you say to a friend considering steroids to improve athletic performance? Use facts from the book.
prompt.zh: 如果有朋友想用类固醇提升运动表现，你会对他说什么？请引用书中的事实。

en: State a clear position against steroid use
Give two health or reputation risks from the book
Suggest a healthier alternative for improvement
zh: 明确表达反对使用类固醇的立场
举出书中提到的两项健康或名誉风险
提出更健康的提升方式
